hypotheses.name: GO enrichment of SV3 poles across 12 layers
hypotheses.method: SVD of 209-gene mean-centered embeddings at each of 12 layers. For SV3 top-K=52 and bottom-K=52 pole genes: Fisher exact test against 594 GO BP+CC terms (size 3-60). Gene-label shuffle null N=300. Report best GO term per pole per layer and empirical p.
hypotheses.result_value: 12/12 layers significant for both top and bottom SV3 poles (all emp_p<=0.017). TOP axis: stress response/kinase (L0-5) -> cell surface (L6) -> DNA binding/chromatin (L9-11). BOT axis: ISR signaling (L0-3) -> inflammatory (L4-5) -> MHC-II/immune (L7-11).
hypotheses.next_action: SV3 encodes a biologically coherent kinase/signaling vs immune axis that transitions with depth. Map top SV3 genes per pole to known cancer/immune pathways. Test if SV3 poles predict drug sensitivity or perturbation response.

hypotheses.name: Layer-depth Spearman trend on SV2/SV3 PPI z-scores
hypotheses.method: Load iter_0013 H01 JSON (SV1/SV2/SV3 co-pole z-scores for 1022 STRING pairs at each of 12 layers). Compute Spearman rho between layer index [0-11] and z-score for each axis. Peak layer identification.
hypotheses.result_value: SV2: rho=-0.559 p=0.059 (peak layer=1, moderate decline with depth). SV3: rho=-0.259 p=0.417 (peak layer=3, non-monotonic bell shape). SV1: rho=0.168 p=0.602 (no trend).
hypotheses.next_action: SV2 geometry peaks in early layers (L1) and moderately weakens toward output. SV3 is non-monotonic (bell peak at L3). Neither is definitively monotonic. Not a strong mechanistic finding. Consider testing if early-layer geometry predicts late-layer geometry.

hypotheses.name: Expanded hub-degree control: non-hub SV2 co-pole enrichment (STRING score>=0.4)
hypotheses.method: Load STRING JSON (score>=0.4 threshold). N_total=3364 edges, N_hub=2347, N_nonhub=138. For each of 12 layers: SVD, SV2 co-pole test (K=52) on hub vs non-hub edges. Gene-label shuffle null N=300 each.
hypotheses.result_value: Non-hub mean z=3.107 (12/12 sig). Hub mean z=2.595 (9/12 sig). Non-hub signal exceeds hub signal. PPI co-pole enrichment is NOT driven by hubs.
hypotheses.next_action: Key finding: PPI geometry in SV2 survives low-degree (non-hub) gene pairs with STRONGER z-scores than hub pairs. This rules out hub-degree confound and strengthens the PPI geometry claim. Add to main paper as null control evidence.
